title: neonauclea puberula
intro: Neonauclea puberula, also known as the Malayan ironwood or Malabar ironwood, is a tree species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic effects, attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Neonauclea puberula include reducing fever, alleviating pain and inflammation, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Neonauclea puberula are typically made by boiling the leaves or bark in water, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
